#### ↳ Re: COBOL Program To Do Bar Codes On OS2

- **From:** maxomtech@wiscasset.net
- **Date:** 1999-01-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,alt.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<785p09$68b$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`
- **References:** `<7832li$quj$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```
Hi Daniel:

There is a product (hardware) that we use, it is called "KWIK-BAR". This
device is in-line with the printer (includes both serial and parallel ports).
 You send text strings, no escape codes, to select printer type, barcode
type, barcode data, along with your normal data and it will "translate" the
commands to produce your barcodes.

This method works for us because, many times, we don't know what type of
environment (Operating system, COBOL Vendor, printers....) our application
will be running in and this gives us the most flexibility without having to
write "printer drivers."  You can actually program it through your print
jobs.  We just setup parameters that the user can modify (the type of
barcodes, the type of printer....).

It sends the appropriate codes for several different printer types (IBM,
EPSON, HP, Oki).

The name of the company that makes the KWIK-BAR is Intelligent Alternatives,
they are somewhere in Alabama, and the phone number escapes me at this moment.

Not sure if this will work for what you need, but I hope it helps.

Chris Loy
Maxom Holdings, Inc.


In article <7832li$quj$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>,
  dcantua@my-dejanews.com wrote:

> need to print out some bar codes on Okidata Okipage 10i laser printer.  We are
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…

```

<a id="t-20a8361e74"></a>
## Thread: Communication Section — 6 msgs

_Topics: [`Language features and syntax`](topics.md#syntax)_

### Communication Section

- **From:** nick <nick@km.ua>
- **Date:** 1999-01-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36A4CE50.1972C836@km.ua>`

```
I am looking for any documents about 'Communication Section' COBOL . We
are programming the Cobol to 'C' converter.

Could you help me with some examples, or point me at any materials about
this.

Nick.
```

#### ↳ Re: Communication Section

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-01-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<782quv$18h@dfw-ixnews6.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<36A4CE50.1972C836@km.ua>`

```
I can tell you one thing about it.  When IBM "dropped support" for it (going
from OS/VS COBOL to VS COBOL II - about '83), "we" at GUIDE tried to find
ANYONE who was actually using it.  It turned out that "the phone company"
(before divestiture) did have one application that was using it.  (On IBM
mainframes, it required TCAM rather than VTAM - hard to find even in the
early '80s).  Several of us at GUIDE were from various "baby bells" - and we
discovered that this application had been "happily running" for years - with
NO ONE at the other end actually using the "output".

Other bits of information:

-  Obviously, it is "optional" in all Standards
-  It is OBSOLETE in the draft of the next Standard
_  Micro Focus (for example) supports (supported) its syntax, but was never
able to "certify" (FIPS) conformance to the Standard - but the test (but not
the Standard) required support for "dumb terminals"
- Last time I asked (several years ago), the only ANSI member who still
certified their compiler for it was Unisys - but I can't remember which
"half"
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Communication Section

- **From:** Scott Ramey <scottr@bdssoftware.com>
- **Date:** 1999-01-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36A54D8B.9823ED33@bdssoftware.com>`
- **References:** `<36A4CE50.1972C836@km.ua> <782quv$18h@dfw-ixnews6.ix.netcom.com>`

```
I did quite a few programs on Burroughs minis which used the communications section.  I was very
happy see the whole obtuse methodology go by the wayside when the programs were converted to Unix. 
Additionally, I always likened maintaining 8,000+ line programs on a dinky machine that took five or
six hours to compile to doing dental work on an elephant -- get it right the first time for God's
sake.  Anyway, the documentation accompanying MF compilers pretty much paralleled the Burroughs
documentation.

"William M. Klein" wrote:
> 
> I can tell you one thing about it.  When IBM "dropped support" for it (going
…[33 more quoted lines elided]…
> >
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Communication Section

- **From:** Barticus@att.spam.net (Randall Bart)
- **Date:** 1999-01-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36a553d1.35114046@netnews>`
- **References:** `<36A4CE50.1972C836@km.ua> <782quv$18h@dfw-ixnews6.ix.netcom.com>`

```
'Twas Tue, 19 Jan 1999 14:46:33 -0600, when "William M. Klein"
<wmklein@nospam.netcom.com> illuminated comp.lang.cobol thusly:

>I can tell you one thing about it.  When IBM "dropped support" for it (going
>from OS/VS COBOL to VS COBOL II - about '83), "we" at GUIDE tried to find
>ANYONE who was actually using it.  

AFAIK, IBM never fully supported it.  They kept pushing users to use their
proprietary CICS interface over the ANSI Cobol Communications Module.  
If IBM doesn't support a standard, it's not a standard anymore.

>_  Micro Focus (for example) supports (supported) its syntax, but was never
>able to "certify" (FIPS) conformance to the Standard - but the test (but not
>the Standard) required support for "dumb terminals"

This sentence overflowed my "but" stack.  Are you you saying that the FIPS
test required support that was not in the standard?

>- Last time I asked (several years ago), the only ANSI member who still
>certified their compiler for it was Unisys - but I can't remember which
>"half"

Unisys A Series COMS follows the X3.23-1974 Communications Module very
closely, and was likely FIPS certified.  I recall that it did not follow
the standard exactly in some details, but I believe it was because no one
ever did.  There were things in Cobol-74 that simply shouldn't be
implemented as written (like the infamous PERFORM ... VARYING ... AFTER
flowchart).
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Communication Section

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-01-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<784dgi$5f8@sjx-ixn6.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<36A4CE50.1972C836@km.ua> <782quv$18h@dfw-ixnews6.ix.netcom.com> <36a553d1.35114046@netnews>`

```
Randall Bart wrote in message <36a553d1.35114046@netnews>...
>'Twas Tue, 19 Jan 1999 14:46:33 -0600, when "William M. Klein"
><wmklein@nospam.netcom.com> illuminated comp.lang.cobol thusly:
>
>>I can tell you one thing about it.  When IBM "dropped support" for it
(going
>>from OS/VS COBOL to VS COBOL II - about '83), "we" at GUIDE tried to find
>>ANYONE who was actually using it.
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>If IBM doesn't support a standard, it's not a standard anymore.


In my opinion, the :*old* IBM support did meet the ANSI Standard ('74 - not
'85) but was certainly NEVER the recommended solution (and did require TCAM
which was "old" before I started programming).  Yes, they prefered CICS or
IMS/DC - but as an application programmer, so did I.

>
>>_  Micro Focus (for example) supports (supported) its syntax, but was
never
>>able to "certify" (FIPS) conformance to the Standard - but the test (but
not
>>the Standard) required support for "dumb terminals"
>
>This sentence overflowed my "but" stack.  Are you you saying that the FIPS
>test required support that was not in the standard?

Yes, I am saying that the FIPS tests required a facility that the ANSI
Standard did not require.

>
>>- Last time I asked (several years ago), the only ANSI member who still
…[17 more quoted lines elided]…
>l    |/ MS^7=6/28/107   http://members.aol.com/PanicYr00/Sequence.html
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Communication Section

- **From:** Albert Ratzlaff <albert@conexion.com.py>
- **Date:** 1999-01-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36A62C8B.93AE5BC2@conexion.com.py>`
- **References:** `<36A4CE50.1972C836@km.ua> <782quv$18h@dfw-ixnews6.ix.netcom.com>`

```
"William M. Klein" wrote:

> I can tell you one thing about it.  When IBM "dropped support" for it (going
> from OS/VS COBOL to VS COBOL II - about '83), "we" at GUIDE tried to find
…[20 more quoted lines elided]…
>     wmklein at ix dot netcom dot com

RM/COBOL provides a 'C' stub program that interfaces to the communication
section. I had a client who migrated from an NCR ITX machine to RM/COBOL on SCO
Unix. The communication section was the backbone of their application - don't
ask me why. They had programs accepting transactions from user terminals
(currency exchanges) which sent the transactions thru the MCS to a background
program which updated all their files. Kind of a spooler. I implemented a basic
MCS system using Unix IPC calls. Was a great opportunity to learn C. Later I
tried to augment the system to do connections over TCP/IP, but the COBOL syntax
is too limited (IMHO); it's easier to use CALLs.

Regards
Albert Ratzlaff
```

<a id="t-e34ac9dc82"></a>
## Thread: Homework, Help — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Tutorials, books, learning`](topics.md#learning)_

### Homework, Help

- **From:** Casey <sarvec@n-link.com>
- **Date:** 1999-01-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36A55626.36B3@n-link.com>`

```
Please don't get on my rear for posting this code. Thane said "post the
code and maybe we can help" a while ago so now I am taking the
opportunity to post it. Is it a coincidence that college started again
today? :-)
Ok, we all know it is my homework and that I am having a hard time with
it. But you can also see that I have worked out a major portion of it on
my own.  I am putting in considerable effort but have decided to turn to
you experts.

The source is first, the input record data is next in [two files] , the
output data is listed last [in two files]. 
The object of the program is to load a table that contains the data for
a pay-table.
There is a hardcoded table that is in there somewhere near the middle.
Process is to read the input record for data, search the table for
corresponding data and if found process the record.
If the data in the input file is not valid [if type-role is not not A
through F] then write an error record and process the next record. There
is also a data check for non-numeric data. If data coming in is not
numeric then write the error record and process the next record
There are a few more calculations and moves to do but I have that
figured out, even though the below code doesn't reflect that.
My main problem is the program trying to read past end of file. 

The source:

IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
 PROGRAM-ID.  PROGRAM8.
 AUTHOR.      CASEY.
 ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
 CONFIGURATION SECTION.
 
 SPECIAL-NAMES.
     C01 IS TOP-OF-PAGE.
 
 INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
 FILE-CONTROL.

     SELECT pay-file ASSIGN TO 'C:\PROGRAM8.DAT'
     organization is line sequential.

     SELECT input-file ASSIGN TO 'C:\xtra-file.DAT'
     ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL.

     select output-file assign to 'c:\prog8out.dat'.
     select error-file assign to 'c:\error.dat'.

 DATA DIVISION.
 FILE SECTION.
 fd  error-file
     label records are omitted
     record contains 80 characters
     data record is error-record
     recording mode is f.

 01  error-record  pic x(80).
    

 FD  pay-file                                               
     LABEL RECORDS ARE OMITTED                                 
     RECORD CONTAINS 28 CHARACTERS
     DATA RECORD IS pay-record
     RECORDING MODE IS F.
 
 01 pay-record.
    05  filler    pic 99v99.

 fd  input-file
     label records are omitted
     record contains 40 characters
     data record is input-record
     recording mode is f.

  01  input-record.
     05  social-number    pic 9(9).
     05  xtras-name       pic x(20).
     05  experience       pic 99.
     05  type-role        pic x.
     05  regular-hours    pic 999v9.
     05  xpanded-role     pic xx.

 fd  output-file
     label records are omitted
     record contains 118 characters
     data record is output-record
     recording mode is f.

 01  output-record    pic x(118).
 
 
 
 working-storage section.
 01  no-more-records   pic xx value spaces.
 01  chart1-index      pic 9  value zero.
 01  chart2-index      pic 9  value zero.
 01  pay-chart-value   pic 9.
 01  movie-pay-file.
     05 pay-row occurs 6 times.
        10  pay-column occurs 7 times.
            15  pay-chart  pic 99v99.

 01  regular-pay       pic 9999v99.
 01  role-type         pic 9.
 01  pay-amount        pic 9999v99.
 01  extra-pay         pic 99V99.
 01  BONUS-INDEXER     pic 9         value zero.
 01  error-switch      pic 99.
 01  wk-regular-hours  pic 9999      value zero.
 01  pay-computer      pic 999v99    value zero.

 01  hard-code-table.
     05  filler        pic x(4)    value   'AA01'. 
     05  filler        pic x(4)    value   'AV01'.
     05  filler   	 pic x(4)    value   'BA03'.
     05  filler   	 pic x(4)    value   'BN05'.
     05  filler   	 pic x(4)    value   'CA05'.
     05  filler   	 pic x(4)    value   'CN04'.
     05  filler   	 pic x(4)    value   'DA08'.
     05  filler   	 pic x(4)    value   'DN08'.
     05  filler   	 pic x(4)    value   'DR09'.
     05  filler   	 pic x(4)    value   'EA14'.
     05  filler  	 pic x(4)    value   'EN03'.
     05  filler  	 pic x(4)    value   'ER03'.
     05  filler  	 pic x(4)    value   'FA01'.
     05  filler  	 pic x(4)    value   'FN06'.

 01  BONUS-TABLE REDEFINES HARD-CODE-TABLE.
     05  BONUS OCCURS 14 TIMES
         ASCENDING KEY IS BONUS-CODE
         INDEXED BY BONUS-INDEX.
         10  BONUS-CODE   pic XX.
         10  BONUS-HOURS  pic 99.
    
 01  HEADING-1.
     05  FILLER           PIC X(9)   VALUE 'SOC SEC #'.
     05  FILLER           PIC X(5)   VALUE SPACE.
     05  FILLER           PIC X(16)  VALUE 'MOVIE EXTRA NAME'.
     05  FILLER           PIC X(5)   VALUE SPACE.
     05  FILLER           PIC X(10)  VALUE 'EXPERIENCE'.
     05  FILLER           PIC X(5)   VALUE SPACE.
     05  FILLER           PIC X(9)   VALUE 'ROLE TYPE'.
     05  FILLER           PIC X(5)   VALUE SPACE.
     05  FILLER           PIC X(12)  VALUE 'HOURLY RATE'.
     05  FILLER           PIC X(5)   VALUE SPACE.
     05  FILLER           PIC X(10)  VALUE 'REG HOURS'.
     05  FILLER           PIC X(5)   VALUE SPACE.
     05  FILLER           PIC X(11)  VALUE 'EXTRA HOURS'.
     05  FILLER           PIC X(5)   VALUE SPACE.
     05  FILLER           PIC X(3)   VALUE 'PAY'.
     
 01  ws-output-record.
     05 ws-social-number  pic 9(9).
     05 filler            pic x(5).
     05 ws-xtras-name     pic x(20).
     05 filler            pic x(5).
     05 ws-experience     pic 99.
     05 filler            pic x(12).
     05 ws-type-role      pic x.
     05 filler            pic x(12).
     05 ws-pay-rate       pic 99.99.
     05 filler            pic x(11).
     05 ws-regular-hours  pic ZZ9.09.
     05 filler            pic x(12).
     05 ws-extra-hours    pic 99.
     05 filler            pic x(8).
     05 ws-pay-amount     pic $$$$$.99.

 01  ws-error-record.
     05 er-social-number  pic 9(9).
     05 filler            pic x(5).
     05 er-xtras-name     pic x(20).
     05 filler            pic x(5).
     05 er-experience     pic 99.
     05 filler            pic x(12).
     05 er-type-role      pic x.

 procedure division.
     open input pay-file input-file  
      output output-file error-file.
       perform load-table-process 
        varying chart1-index from 1 by 1 
         until chart1-index > 6
          after chart2-index from 1 by 1 
           until chart2-index > 7.

     WRITE OUTPUT-RECORD FROM HEADING-1
     AFTER ADVANCING TOP-OF-PAGE.
     read input-file.
     perform process-records until no-more-records = 'no'.

     close input-file error-file pay-file output-file.
     stop run.
 process-records.
     perform type-role-process.
     if type-role = 'G' then 
     perform write-error-records-process
     end-if.
     perform search-process.
     perform compute-hours-process.
     perform move-process.
     display regular-hours.
     move spaces to output-record.
     write output-record from ws-output-record.
     read input-file at end move 'no' to no-more-records.

 write-error-records-process.
     display 'Movie Role Type was not found'.
     display 'writing error record'.
     move social-number to er-social-number.
     move xtras-name to er-xtras-name.
     move experience to er-experience.
     move type-role to er-type-role.
     write error-record from ws-error-record
     after advancing 1 line.
     read input-file at end move 'no' to no-more-records.

 load-table-process. 
     
     read pay-file into pay-chart 
          (chart1-index, chart2-index).
     
     display pay-chart (chart1-index, chart2-index).
* Displays have confirmed that table is properly loaded.     
* display ' '.

 type-role-process.

     move 0 to error-switch.
     move 0 to chart1-index.
      evaluate type-role
      when 'A' move 1 to chart1-index
      when 'B' move 2 to chart1-index
      when 'C' move 3 to chart1-index
      when 'D' move 4 to chart1-index
      when 'E' move 5 to chart1-index
      when 'F' move 6 to chart1-index
     end-evaluate.
     

 experience-process.
     move 0 to chart2-index.
     move 0 to pay-computer.
     evaluate experience
      when 0 move 1 to chart2-index
      when 1 move 2 to chart2-index
      when 2 move 3 to chart2-index
      when 3 move 4 to chart2-index
      when 4 move 5 to chart2-index
      when 5 through 7 move 6 to chart2-index
      when 8 through 15 move 7 to chart2-index
     end-evaluate.
     if experience is not numeric 
     perform write-error-records-process
     end-if.     

     display 'pay chart ' pay-chart (chart1-index, 
       chart2-index).
     
     move pay-chart (chart1-index, chart2-index) to 
      pay-computer ws-pay-rate.

     display ws-pay-rate.
     
 move-process.    
*     add regular-hours to wk-regular-hours.
    

     move social-number to ws-social-number.
     move xtras-name to ws-xtras-name.
     move experience to ws-experience.
     move type-role to ws-type-role.
    

 search-process.
     set bonus-index to 0.
     search all BONUS
      at end move 99 to error-switch
       when BONUS-CODE (BONUS-INDEX) equals xpanded-role
        add bonus-hours (bonus-index) to  wk-regular-hours.
         move bonus-hours (bonus-index) to ws-extra-hours.
     if bonus-code (bonus-index) not equal xpanded-role
      move 00 to ws-extra-hours
     end-if.

 compute-hours-process.
     display 'regular hours: '  regular-hours.
     display 'pay-computer: '  pay-computer.
     move regular-hours to ws-regular-hours.
     add ws-extra-hours to regular-hours.
     compute pay-amount = regular-hours * pay-computer.
     move pay-amount to ws-pay-amount.
     
The input data:

000000002JONES, ROY          02F0450FA

1400170018001900210023002400
1700175018001800185018501900

1375145015001500152515501550

EXTRA HOURS PAY
```

#### ↳ Re: Homework, Help

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-01-19T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<783nej$n8j@dfw-ixnews10.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<36A55626.36B3@n-link.com>`

```
You'll may get lots of comments on this, but a few things to say:

On your "reading past file" question,
    look at your
         process-records
paragraph, and follow the logic *IF* the very last record that you read in
HAPPENS to end up with a type-role = G.

HINT: always be "wary" if your application reads the same file in more than
one place.

   ****

Now having said that (which I think may be your real problem) let me mention
a few other related hints.

When reading a file, NEVER believe the people who have told you how many
records it will have.  You don't have an "AT END" on your first read of the
"input-file" and NONE on your reads of the "pay-file".  Related to this,
when you are loading your pay-table, you do a PERFORM VARYING that doesn't
allow for any "get out" - just in case it doesn't have all the required
records in it.

I hope this helps.

FYI - in the future, if you can "cut your program down" a little to
demonstrate the problem, or provide it as an attachment (2nd choice) or give
an email address (or web page) where everyone can download it, that probably
will work better than posting the whole code.  HOWEVER, we really do respond
better with too much code than with too little.
```

```
sort people used before 1990.
```

## Thread: Replace GO TO w/perform Question — 17 this month / 18 total

_Topics: [`Language features and syntax`](topics.md#syntax)_

### Replace GO TO w/perform Question

- **From:** codetrader@aol.com (Codetrader)
- **Date:** 1999-01-21T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<19990121041129.04344.00000491@ng-fc1.aol.com>`

```
Hi-

Q. What would be an effective way to replace this  GO TO with a PERFORM ?
     My snag is that the GO TO in this example ends this section.  I have
     seen examples where you PERFORM somethig, but if you pass the test
     (IF) and a GO TO then ends the section, how could a perform or logic
     change allow for the next test, and/or the MOVE that follows the first
test?
     I am not allowed to use GO TO.
Thanks in advance
    - Jake


example: 
PROCEDURE DIVISION.

PERFORM 100-VALIDATE-THIS-DATA THRU DATA-VALIDATED.

100-VALIDATE-THIS-DATA.
      IF THIS-PIECE IS ALPHABETIC
      GO TO 100-DATA VALIDATED.
      MOVE ...........
      IF THIS-PIECE IS NUMERIC
       MOVE THIS PIECE TO VLD-PIECE.
100-DATA VALIDATED.
```

#### ↳ Re: Replace GO TO w/perform Question

- **From:** JeffreyFarkas@earthlink.net (Jeff Farkas)
- **Date:** 1999-01-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<MPG.110fb1c31c9b405b98968e@news.earthlink.net>`
- **References:** `<19990121041129.04344.00000491@ng-fc1.aol.com>`

```
In article <19990121041129.04344.00000491@ng-
fc1.aol.com>, codetrader@aol.com says...
<Snip>

Hi Jake,

    I agree with Judson's answer to this question. It 
seems that the best way to approach this is to make use 
of a GO TO statement and transfer control to the end of 
the section of code. 

    In many cases you will have multiple edits to 
perform on a particular data item and the "best" way to 
attack it is to locate all of the edits in one section 
of code and perform each test one at a time. When one of 
them fails use a GO TO and get out of that section of 
logic. 

     While use of a GO TO can lead to some problems it 
is a very acceptable way to code large sections of logic 
and not fragment them into dozens of small pieces that 
become hard to related at a later date. 

     Just my 2 cents on the topic since you can't use a 
GO TO in you program you will need to break up the logic 
into small paragraphs and/or use some other method to 
get around the use of a GO TO.

Jeff



> example: 
> PROCEDURE DIVISION.
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

#### ↳ Re: Replace GO TO w/perform Question

- **From:** "Judson McClendon" <judmc123@bellsouth.net>
- **Date:** 1999-01-21T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<TMFp2.50$jE1.40956@news4.mia>`
- **References:** `<19990121041129.04344.00000491@ng-fc1.aol.com>`

```
Jake,

I triggered a *long* thread on this several months ago.  There are three
general approaches to removing the GO TO in your example.  1. Move the
remainder of the paragraph into a separate paragraph and perform it in
the inverse of the GO TO conditional.  2. Set some kind of 'error flag'
and test it in every following statement in the paragraph.  3. In COBOL
85 you can embed the remainder of the paragraph between ELSE/END IF.

To my thinking, neither of these alternatives is to be desired.  The
first approach would mecessitate breaking a large, contiguous paragraph
up into many smaller ones in cases where there were a number of such
GO TO's.  The second approach adds a host of otherwize unnessary 'IF'
tests in the remainder of the paragraph, unduly obfuscating the logic
in the process.  The third method works fine for small paragraphs, but
breaks down into deeply nested, obfuscated code on long paragraphs with
many such points.  The proposed standard will have the EXIT PARAGRAPH
statement, which does the same thing without the GO TO or exit paragraph,
making this question a moot point.  Until then, I much prefer the simple
use of PERFORM ... THRU ...-EXIT, and GO TO ...-EXIT to the slavish
avoidance of GO TO at all costs, under any circumstances.  In my opinion,
the only real disadvantage of a GO TO ...-EXIT as used here is that it
presents an opportunity for a programming error when a GO TO is made
to a paragraph other than the proper exit.  Personally, I use a simple
code checked to eliminate this possibility.  After all, the EXIT
PARAGRAPH statement is effectively a GO TO which can only be to the
paragraph exit; identical in function, use and limitations to GO TO
...-EXIT when used with the code checker.

Get braced, for you will no doubt receive a flurry of rebuttals to my
position. :-)
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Replace GO TO w/perform Question

- **From:** Albert Ratzlaff <albert@conexion.com.py>
- **Date:** 1999-01-22T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36A8454D.2B270AEF@conexion.com.py>`
- **References:** `<19990121041129.04344.00000491@ng-fc1.aol.com> <TMFp2.50$jE1.40956@news4.mia>`

```
Judson McClendon wrote:

> Get braced, for you will no doubt receive a flurry of rebuttals to my
> position. :-)

Here's the first!
(just kidding :-)

I have in my hands a copy of "Writings of the Revolution - Selected Readings on
Software Engineering", edited by Edward Jourdon. It contains "Notes on Avoiding
GO TO Statements", by Knuth and Floyd. The general spirit of the paper, as I
understand it, is to not concentrate on avoiding GO TOs, but on coding in the
most clear and efficient way. If you can do that better by using GO TO, then by
all means, do it.

There are some interesting examples in the paper - and you all know how thorough
Knuth is when he writes something!

Regards
Albert Ratzlaff
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Replace GO TO w/perform Question

- **From:** "Dennis J. Minette" <dennis_minette@email.msn.com>
- **Date:** 1999-01-28T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ObdG2OpS#GA.172@upnetnews03>`
- **References:** `<19990121041129.04344.00000491@ng-fc1.aol.com> <TMFp2.50$jE1.40956@news4.mia>`

```

Judson McClendon wrote in message ...
>Jake,
>
…[28 more quoted lines elided]…
>position. :-)


Not from me!  Judicious use of GO TO, restricted as you have outlined, is
IMHO preferable to most of the alternatives.  I believe GO TO should be used
ONLY to exit a paragraph invoked in a PERFORM paragraph-name THRU
paragraph-name-exit construct, where paragraph-name-exit contains only EXIT.
Even this usage will be obviated upon the adoption of the EXIT-PARAGRAPH
construct in the COBOL Standard of the next millennium.

>Judson McClendon      judmc123@bellsouth.net  (remove numbers)
>Sun Valley Systems    http://personal.bhm.bellsouth.net/~judmc
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
>>     change allow for the next test, and/or the MOVE that follows the
first
>>test?
>>     I am not allowed to use GO TO.
…[18 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

#### ↳ Re: Replace GO TO w/perform Question

- **From:** Howard Brazee <NOSPAMbrazee@home.com>
- **Date:** 1999-01-21T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36A73A76.D8CFE823@home.com>`
- **References:** `<19990121041129.04344.00000491@ng-fc1.aol.com>`

```
The way to avoid using GO TO is to design your program properly to begin
with.  However, sometimes we are given code designed for GO TO and told
to remove the GO TO's.  See below.

Codetrader wrote:
> 
> Hi-
…[22 more quoted lines elided]…
> 100-DATA VALIDATED.

The easiest solution (not the best one - the best one is more of a
rewrite) is to do the following:
> 100-VALIDATE-THIS-DATA.
>       IF THIS-PIECE IS not ALPHABETIC
            perform 110-continue validation.

  110-continue-validation.
>***    GO TO 100-DATA VALIDATED.
>       MOVE ...........
>       IF THIS-PIECE IS NUMERIC
>        MOVE THIS PIECE TO VLD-PIECE.
> 100-DATA VALIDATED.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Replace GO TO w/perform Question

- **From:** "COBOL Frog (Huib Klink)" <H.Klink@IMN.nl>
- **Date:** 1999-01-22T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36A856C8.25B6F6D9@IMN.nl>`
- **References:** `<19990121041129.04344.00000491@ng-fc1.aol.com> <36A73A76.D8CFE823@home.com>`

```
Howard Brazee wrote:

> Codetrader wrote:
> > PERFORM 100-VALIDATE-THIS-DATA THRU DATA-VALIDATED.
…[20 more quoted lines elided]…
> > 100-DATA VALIDATED.

But then the perform should also be changed and become:
    PERFORM 100-VALIDATE-THIS-DATA
in other words: the THRU-part is left off, otherwise the 110-continue-validation
would always be executed, once for alphabetic and twice for non-alphabetic
this-pieces.

The Frog
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Replace GO TO w/perform Question

- **From:** Howard Brazee <NOSPAMbrazee@home.com>
- **Date:** 1999-01-22T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36A891D8.4D31A73@home.com>`
- **References:** `<19990121041129.04344.00000491@ng-fc1.aol.com> <36A73A76.D8CFE823@home.com> <36A856C8.25B6F6D9@IMN.nl>`

```
> But then the perform should also be changed and become:
>     PERFORM 100-VALIDATE-THIS-DATA
> in other words: the THRU-part is left off, otherwise the 110-continue-validation
> would always be executed, once for alphabetic and twice for non-alphabetic
> this-pieces.

Right.  If I had my way, Procedure division sections and the word THRU
would go the way of ALTER.  Too many maintenance problems.  Let each
perform do a single function.
```

```
> Hi-

> PROCEDURE DIVISION.
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
>

I would code this little piece of code the following way:

 PERFORM 100-VALIDATE-THIS-DATA
 IF ERROR-FLAG = 'S' OR ERROR-FLAG = 'E'
  THEN PERFORM 999-ERROR
 END-IF

 100-VALIDATE-THIS-DATA SECTION.
       IF THIS-PIECE IS ALPHABETIC
        THEN
         MOVE ...........
        ELSE
         IF THIS-PIECE IS NUMERIC
          THEN
           MOVE THIS-PIECE TO VLD-PIECE
          ELSE
           MOVE 'S' TO ERROR-FLAG
           MOVE 'INVALID DATA IN THIS-PIECE' TO ERROR-DIAGNOSTIC
         END-IF
       END-IF
      .

Or maybe I would use the EVALUATE-Statement in this case (only the section
part):

 100-VALIDATE-THIS-DATA SECTION.
      EVALUATE TRUE
       WHEN THIS-PIECE IS ALPHABETIC
         MOVE ...........
       WHEN THIS-PIECE IS NUMERIC
         MOVE THIS PIECE TO VLD-PIECE
       WHEN OTHER
         MOVE 'S' TO ERROR-FLAG
         MOVE 'INVALID DATA IN THIS-PIECE' TO ERROR-DIAGNOSTIC
      END-EVALUATE
      .
Daniel


-----------== Posted via Deja News, The Discussion Network ==----------
http://www.dejanews.com/       Search, Read, Discuss, or Start Your Own
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Replace GO TO w/perform Question

- **From:** Barticus@att.spam.net (Randall Bart)
- **Date:** 1999-01-21T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36a795ab.11233535@netnews>`
- **References:** `<19990121041129.04344.00000491@ng-fc1.aol.com> <787jgf$npj$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```
'Twas Thu, 21 Jan 1999 16:10:01 GMT, when daniel.jacot@winterthur.ch
illuminated comp.lang.cobol thusly:

> 100-VALIDATE-THIS-DATA SECTION.
>       IF THIS-PIECE IS ALPHABETIC

In Cobol-85 you need a paragraph header between the section header and a
statement.  This requirement is relaxed in the next standard.

Ramaniuk had the best answer to the original question.
```

- **From:** Alex Romaniuk <ales.romaniuk@zag.si>

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36A76372.7893@zag.si>`
- **References:** `<19990121041129.04344.00000491@ng-fc1.aol.com>`

```
Codetrader wrote:
> example:
> PROCEDURE DIVISION.
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
> 100-DATA VALIDATED.

  *%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%% 
   100-VALIDATE-THIS-DATA.
  *%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%% 
       IF THIS-PIECE NOT ALPHABETIC
          MOVE .....
          IF THIS-PIECE IS NUMERIC
             MOVE THIS-PIECE TO VLD-PIECE
          END-IF
       END-IF
```

<a id="t-d472e78a2b"></a>
## Thread: Validation Prog. Homework - Help! — 15 msgs

_Topics: [`Tutorials, books, learning`](topics.md#learning)_

### Validation Prog. Homework - Help!

- **From:** "���" <no@spam.com>
- **Date:** 1999-01-21T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<x6up2.1767$PD.10339027@news.magma.ca>`

```
Hello,

I'm doing an assignment which requires me to validate records from a
software company.  My problem is in the "Version Number".  In the test data,
one of the 'error records' contains a letter in the version # (i.e. version
A00 instead of 100).  So, in my output I have to display the version number
as, for example version " 1.00 " , but with the error one I can't move "
A.00 " to my numeric field because it's alphanumeric.  I hope I explained
this well enough ;)  I'll also include my code and the input record if it
helps.  Any help would be GREATLY appreciated as I've been stuck with this
problem for the last week!  Thank you, Steve

Here's the code:

       DATA DIVISION.
       FILE SECTION.

       FD PRODUCT-DIST-FILE
           RECORD CONTAINS 56 CHARACTERS.
       01 PRODUCT-DIST-RECORD.
           05 PD-PRODUCT-NAME              PIC X(25).

           05 PD-PRODUCT-VERSION-X-1       PIC X.
           05 PD-PRODUCT-VERSION-N-1 REDEFINES
               PD-PRODUCT-VERSION-X-1      PIC 9.

           05 PD-PRODUCT-VERSION-X-2       PIC XX.
           05 PD-PRODUCT-VERSION-N-2 REDEFINES
               PD-PRODUCT-VERSION-X-2      PIC 99.

           05 PD-RELEASE-DATE.
              10 PD-RELEASE-DATE-YEAR-X    PIC X(4).
              10 PD-RELEASE-DATE-YEAR-N REDEFINES PD-RELEASE-DATE-YEAR-X
                                           PIC 9(4).
              10 PD-RELEASE-DATE-MONTH-N   PIC 9(2).
               88 VALID-MONTH              VALUES 1 THRU 12.
               88 FEBRUARY                 VALUE 2.
               88 30-DAY-MONTH             VALUES 4 6 9 11.
               88 31-DAY-MONTH             VALUES 1 3 5 7 8 10 12.
              10 PD-RELEASE-DATE-MONTH-X REDEFINES
                   PD-RELEASE-DATE-MONTH-N PIC X(2).
              10 PD-RELEASE-DATE-DAY-X     PIC X(2).
              10 PD-RELEASE-DATE-DAY-N REDEFINES
                   PD-RELEASE-DATE-DAY-X   PIC 9(2).
           05 PD-NUMERIC-REL-DATE REDEFINES PD-RELEASE-DATE PIC 9(8).

           05 PD-NUM-LICENSES-SOLD-X       PIC X(4).
           05 PD-NUM-LICENSES-SOLD-N REDEFINES
               PD-NUM-LICENSES-SOLD-X      PIC 9(4).

           05 PD-PRODUCT-SALES-X           PIC X(8).
           05 PD-PRODUCT-SALES-N REDEFINES
               PD-PRODUCT-SALES-X          PIC 9(8).

           05 PD-DEVELOPMENT-COST-X        PIC X(8).
           05 PD-DEVELOPMENT-COST-N REDEFINES
               PD-DEVELOPMENT-COST-X       PIC 9(8).

       FD VALID-PRODUCT-DIST-FILE
           RECORD CONTAINS 56 CHARACTERS.
       01 VALID-PRODUCT-DIST-RECORD        PIC X(56).

       FD VALIDATION-AUDIT-REPORT
           RECORD CONTAINS 80 CHARACTERS.
       01 VALIDATION-AUDIT-RECORD          PIC X(150).

       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.

       01 WS-PROGRAM-SWITCHES.
           05 WS-EOF-SWITCH                PIC X(3).
              88 WS-EOF                    VALUE "YES".

       01 WS-TODAYS-DATE.
           05 WS-TODAYS-YEAR               PIC 9(4).
           05 WS-TODAYS-MONTH              PIC 9(2).
           05 WS-TODAYS-DAY                PIC 9(2).

       01 WS-PAGE-CONTROLS.
           05 WS-MAX-LINES-PER-PAGE        PIC 99 VALUE 25.
           05 WS-CURRENT-PAGE-NUMBER       PIC 99.
           05 WS-CURRENT-LINE-NUMBER       PIC 99.
           05 WS-LINE-SPACING              PIC 9.

       01 WS-RECORD-COUNTERS.
           05 WS-INPUT-RECORD-COUNTER      PIC 999.
           05 WS-VALID-RECORD-COUNTER      PIC 999.
           05 WS-ERROR-RECORD-COUNTER      PIC 999.

       01 WS-RECORD-SWITCHES.
           05 WS-VALID-RECORD-SWITCH       PIC X(3).

       01 WS-CONSTANTS.
           05 WS-MINIMUM-YEAR              PIC 9(4) VALUE 1995.
           05 WS-MAXIMUM-YEAR              PIC 9(4) VALUE 2005.
           05 WS-BETA                      PIC 9    VALUE 1.

       01 ERROR-MESSAGES.
           05 PROD-NAME-MISSING-ERR        PIC X(23)
               VALUE "PRODUCT NAME is missing".
           05 PROD-VERSION-MISSING-ERR     PIC X(26)
               VALUE "PRODUCT VERSION is missing".
           05 PROD-VERSION-NOT-NUM-ERR     PIC X(30)
               VALUE "PRODUCT VERSION is not numeric".

           05 RELEASE-DATE-MISSING-ERR     PIC X(23)
               VALUE "RELEASE DATE is missing".
           05 RELEASE-DATE-INCOMPLETE-ERR  PIC X(10)
               VALUE "incomplete".
           05 RELEASE-DATE-NOT-NUM-ERR     PIC X(28)
               VALUE "RELEASE DATE is not numberic".
           05 YEAR-INVALID-ERR             PIC X(15)
               VALUE "YEAR is invalid".
           05 MONTH-INVALID-ERR            PIC X(16)
               VALUE "MONTH is invalid".
           05 DAY-INVALID-ERR              PIC X(14)
               VALUE "DAY is invalid".

           05 NUMBER-SOLD-MISSING-ERR      PIC X(22)
               VALUE "NUMBER SOLD is missing".
           05 NUMBER-SOLD-NOT-NUM-ERR      PIC X(26)
               VALUE "NUMBER SOLD is not numeric".
           05 NUMBER-SOLD-IN-FUTURE-ERR    PIC X(28)
               VALUE "NUMBER SOLD is in the future".

           05 PROD-SALES-INVALID-ERR       PIC X(30)
               VALUE "PRODUCT SALES invalid for Beta".
           05 PROD-SALES-NOT-FUTURE-ERR    PIC X(33)
               VALUE "PRODUCT SALES cannot be in future".
           05 PROD-SALES-NOT-NUM-ERR       PIC X(28)
               VALUE "PRODUCT SALES is not numeric".

           05 DEV-COST-MISSING-ERR         PIC X(27)
               VALUE "DEVELOPMENT COST is missing".
           05 DEV-COST-NOT-NUM-ERR         PIC X(31)
               VALUE "DEVELOPMENT COST is not numeric".

       01 HDG-1.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(5)    VALUE "DATE:".
           05 FILLER                    PIC X       VALUE SPACES.
           05 HDG-DATE.
               10 HDG-DAY               PIC 99/.
               10 HDG-MONTH             PIC 99/.
               10 HDG-YEAR              PIC 9999.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(53)   VALUE SPACES.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(5)    VALUE "PAGE:".
           05 FILLER                    PIC X       VALUE SPACES.
           05 HDG-PAGE-NUMBER           PIC Z9.

       01 HDG-2.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(24)   VALUE SPACES.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(38)
                   VALUE "PRODUCT DISTRIBUTION VALIDATION REPORT".

       01 HDG-3.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(12)
           VALUE "PRODUCT NAME".
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(10)   VALUE SPACES.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(7)    VALUE "VERSION".
           05 FILLER                    PIC X       VALUE SPACES.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(7)    VALUE "RELEASE".
           05 FILLER                    PIC X       VALUE SPACES.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(4)    VALUE "DATE".
           05 FILLER                    PIC X       VALUE SPACES.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X       VALUE "#".
           05 FILLER                    PIC X       VALUE SPACES.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(4)    VALUE "SOLD".
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(3)    VALUE SPACES.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X       VALUE "$".
           05 FILLER                    PIC X       VALUE SPACES.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(5)    VALUE "SALES".
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(3)    VALUE SPACES.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X       VALUE "$".
           05 FILLER                    PIC X       VALUE SPACES.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(4)    VALUE "COST".
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(6)    VALUE SPACES.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(13)
           VALUE "ERROR MESSAGE".

       01 DETAIL-LINE.
           05 DL-PRODUCT-NAME           PIC X(25).
           05 FILLER                    PIC X       VALUE SPACES.
           05 DL-PRODUCT-VERSION-1      PIC 9.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X       VALUE ".".
           05 DL-PRODUCT-VERSION-2      PIC 99.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(3)    VALUE SPACES.
           05 DL-RELEASE-DATE-DAY       PIC 9(2).
           05 FILLER                    PIC X       VALUE "/".
           05 DL-RELEASE-DATE-MONTH     PIC 9(2).
           05 FILLER                    PIC X       VALUE "/".
           05 DL-RELEASE-DATE-YEAR      PIC 9(4).
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(3)    VALUE SPACES.
           05 DL-NUM-LICENSES-SOLD      PIC ZZZ9.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(2)    VALUE SPACES.
           05 DL-PRODUCT-SALES          PIC $$$$$$$$9-.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(2)    VALUE SPACES.
           05 DL-DEVELOPMENT-COST       PIC $$$$$$$$9-.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(3)    VALUE SPACES.
           05 DL-ERR-MESSAGE            PIC X(35).

       01 ERR-DETAIL-LINE.
           05 ER-PRODUCT-NAME           PIC X(25).
           05 FILLER                    PIC X       VALUE SPACES.
           05 ER-PRODUCT-VERSION-1      PIC X.
           05 FILLER-ER-1               PIC X       VALUE ".".
           05 ER-PRODUCT-VERSION-2      PIC XX.
           05 FILLER-ER-2               PIC X(3)    VALUE SPACES.
           05 ER-RELEASE-DATE-DAY       PIC X(2).
           05 FILLER-ER-3               PIC X       VALUE "/".
           05 ER-RELEASE-DATE-MONTH     PIC X(2).
           05 FILLER-ER-4               PIC X       VALUE "/".
           05 ER-RELEASE-DATE-YEAR      PIC X(4).
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(3)    VALUE SPACES.
           05 ER-NUM-LICENSES-SOLD      PIC XXXX.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(2)    VALUE SPACES.
           05 ER-PRODUCT-SALES          PIC X(8).
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(2)    VALUE SPACES.
           05 ER-DEVELOPMENT-COST       PIC X(8).
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(3)    VALUE SPACES.
           05 ER-ERR-MESSAGE            PIC X(35).


       01 TOTAL-LINE-1.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(3)    VALUE SPACES.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(20)
           VALUE "TOTAL INPUT RECORDS:".
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(2)    VALUE SPACES.
           05 TL-INPUT-RECORD-COUNTER   PIC ZZ9.

       01 TOTAL-LINE-2.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(3)    VALUE SPACES.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(20)
           VALUE "TOTAL VALID RECORDS:".
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(2)    VALUE SPACES.
           05 TL-VALID-RECORD-COUNTER   PIC ZZ9.

       01 TOTAL-LINE-3.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(3)    VALUE SPACES.
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(20)
           VALUE "TOTAL ERROR RECORDS:".
           05 FILLER                    PIC X(2)    VALUE SPACES.
           05 TL-ERROR-RECORD-COUNTER   PIC ZZ9.

       PROCEDURE DIVISION.

       000-SOFTWARE-VALIDATION.
           PERFORM A100-STARTUP
           PERFORM A200-PROCESSING
               UNTIL WS-EOF
           PERFORM A300-WRAPUP
           STOP RUN.

       A100-STARTUP.
           PERFORM B100-OPEN-PRODUCT-DIST-FILES
           PERFORM B200-GET-SYSTEM-DATE
           PERFORM B300-INITIALIZE-VARIABLES
           PERFORM B450-PRINT-HEADINGS
           PERFORM B700-READ-PRODUCT-DIST-RECORD.

       A200-PROCESSING.
           IF WS-CURRENT-LINE-NUMBER >= WS-MAX-LINES-PER-PAGE
               PERFORM B450-PRINT-HEADINGS
           ELSE
               CONTINUE
           END-IF.
           PERFORM B400-INIT-REC-VARIABLES
           PERFORM B500-VALIDATE-PRODUCT-REC
           PERFORM B600-WRITE-VALID-RECORD
           PERFORM B700-READ-PRODUCT-DIST-RECORD.

       A300-WRAPUP.
           PERFORM B800-WRITE-TOTAL-LINES
           PERFORM B900-CLOSE-FILES.

       B100-OPEN-PRODUCT-DIST-FILES.
           OPEN INPUT PRODUCT-DIST-FILE
                OUTPUT VALID-PRODUCT-DIST-FILE
                       VALIDATION-AUDIT-REPORT.

       B200-GET-SYSTEM-DATE.
           ACCEPT WS-TODAYS-DATE FROM DATE YYYYMMDD.
           MOVE WS-TODAYS-YEAR TO HDG-YEAR.
           MOVE WS-TODAYS-MONTH TO HDG-MONTH.
           MOVE WS-TODAYS-DAY TO HDG-DAY.

       B300-INITIALIZE-VARIABLES.
           MOVE SPACES TO WS-EOF-SWITCH.
           INITIALIZE WS-RECORD-COUNTERS.
           MOVE ZERO TO WS-CURRENT-PAGE-NUMBER.
           MOVE ZERO TO WS-CURRENT-LINE-NUMBER.
           MOVE 2 TO WS-LINE-SPACING.

       B400-INIT-REC-VARIABLES.
           MOVE "YES" TO WS-VALID-RECORD-SWITCH.

           MOVE PD-PRODUCT-NAME TO DL-PRODUCT-NAME.
           MOVE PD-PRODUCT-NAME TO ER-PRODUCT-NAME.
           MOVE PD-PRODUCT-VERSION-N-1 TO DL-PRODUCT-VERSION-1.
           MOVE PD-PRODUCT-VERSION-N-2 TO DL-PRODUCT-VERSION-2.
           MOVE PD-PRODUCT-VERSION-X-1 TO ER-PRODUCT-VERSION-1.
           MOVE PD-PRODUCT-VERSION-X-2 TO ER-PRODUCT-VERSION-2.
           MOVE PD-RELEASE-DATE-DAY-N TO DL-RELEASE-DATE-DAY.
           MOVE PD-RELEASE-DATE-DAY-X TO ER-RELEASE-DATE-DAY.
           MOVE PD-RELEASE-DATE-MONTH-N TO DL-RELEASE-DATE-MONTH.
           MOVE PD-RELEASE-DATE-MONTH-X TO ER-RELEASE-DATE-MONTH.
           MOVE PD-RELEASE-DATE-YEAR-N TO DL-RELEASE-DATE-YEAR.
           MOVE PD-RELEASE-DATE-YEAR-X TO ER-RELEASE-DATE-YEAR.
           MOVE PD-NUM-LICENSES-SOLD-N TO DL-NUM-LICENSES-SOLD.
           MOVE PD-NUM-LICENSES-SOLD-X TO ER-NUM-LICENSES-SOLD.
           MOVE PD-PRODUCT-SALES-N TO DL-PRODUCT-SALES.
           MOVE PD-PRODUCT-SALES-X TO ER-PRODUCT-SALES.
           MOVE PD-DEVELOPMENT-COST-N TO DL-DEVELOPMENT-COST.
           MOVE PD-DEVELOPMENT-COST-X TO ER-DEVELOPMENT-COST.
           MOVE 2 TO WS-LINE-SPACING.

       B450-PRINT-HEADINGS.
           MOVE ZERO TO WS-CURRENT-LINE-NUMBER
           ADD 1 TO WS-CURRENT-PAGE-NUMBER
           MOVE WS-CURRENT-PAGE-NUMBER TO HDG-PAGE-NUMBER
           MOVE SPACES TO VALIDATION-AUDIT-RECORD
               WRITE VALIDATION-AUDIT-RECORD
               AFTER ADVANCING PAGE
           MOVE HDG-1 TO VALIDATION-AUDIT-RECORD
               WRITE VALIDATION-AUDIT-RECORD
               AFTER ADVANCING 2 LINES
               ADD 2 TO WS-CURRENT-LINE-NUMBER
           MOVE HDG-2 TO VALIDATION-AUDIT-RECORD
               WRITE VALIDATION-AUDIT-RECORD
               AFTER ADVANCING 2 LINES
               ADD 2 TO WS-CURRENT-LINE-NUMBER
           MOVE HDG-3 TO VALIDATION-AUDIT-RECORD
               WRITE VALIDATION-AUDIT-RECORD
               AFTER ADVANCING 3 LINES
               ADD 3 TO WS-CURRENT-LINE-NUMBER.

       B500-VALIDATE-PRODUCT-REC.
           PERFORM C100-VALIDATE-PROD-NAME.
           PERFORM C200-VALIDATE-PROD-VERSION.
           PERFORM C300-VALIDATE-RELEASE-DATE.
           PERFORM C400-VALIDATE-NUM-LIC-SOLD.
           PERFORM C500-VALIDATE-PROD-SALES.
           PERFORM C600-VALIDATE-DEVEL-COSTS.

       B600-WRITE-VALID-RECORD.
           IF WS-VALID-RECORD-SWITCH = "YES" THEN
               WRITE VALIDATION-AUDIT-RECORD FROM DETAIL-LINE
                   AFTER ADVANCING WS-LINE-SPACING
               ADD WS-LINE-SPACING TO WS-CURRENT-LINE-NUMBER
               WRITE VALID-PRODUCT-DIST-RECORD FROM PRODUCT-DIST-RECORD
               ADD 1 TO WS-VALID-RECORD-COUNTER
           ELSE
               PERFORM B650-WRITE-ERROR-RECORD
           END-IF.

       B650-WRITE-ERROR-RECORD.
               WRITE VALIDATION-AUDIT-RECORD FROM ERR-DETAIL-LINE
               AFTER ADVANCING WS-LINE-SPACING
               ADD WS-LINE-SPACING TO WS-CURRENT-LINE-NUMBER
               ADD 1 TO WS-ERROR-RECORD-COUNTER.



       B700-READ-PRODUCT-DIST-RECORD.
           READ PRODUCT-DIST-FILE
               AT END
                   MOVE "YES" TO WS-EOF-SWITCH
               NOT AT END
                   ADD 1 TO WS-INPUT-RECORD-COUNTER
               END-READ.

       B800-WRITE-TOTAL-LINES.
           MOVE WS-INPUT-RECORD-COUNTER TO TL-INPUT-RECORD-COUNTER
           MOVE WS-VALID-RECORD-COUNTER TO TL-VALID-RECORD-COUNTER
           MOVE WS-ERROR-RECORD-COUNTER TO TL-ERROR-RECORD-COUNTER
           MOVE TOTAL-LINE-1 TO VALIDATION-AUDIT-RECORD
           WRITE VALIDATION-AUDIT-RECORD
               AFTER ADVANCING 3 LINES.
           MOVE TOTAL-LINE-2 TO VALIDATION-AUDIT-RECORD
           WRITE VALIDATION-AUDIT-RECORD
               AFTER ADVANCING 2 LINES.
           MOVE TOTAL-LINE-3 TO VALIDATION-AUDIT-RECORD
           WRITE VALIDATION-AUDIT-RECORD
               AFTER ADVANCING 2 LINES.

       B900-CLOSE-FILES.
           CLOSE PRODUCT-DIST-FILE
                 VALID-PRODUCT-DIST-FILE
                 VALIDATION-AUDIT-REPORT.

       C100-VALIDATE-PROD-NAME.
           IF PD-PRODUCT-NAME = SPACES
               MOVE PROD-NAME-MISSING-ERR TO DL-ERR-MESSAGE
               PERFORM D100-WRITE-ERROR-LINE
           END-IF.

       C200-VALIDATE-PROD-VERSION.
           IF PD-PRODUCT-VERSION-X-1 = SPACES
              OR PD-PRODUCT-VERSION-X-2 = SPACES
               MOVE PROD-VERSION-MISSING-ERR TO DL-ERR-MESSAGE
               PERFORM D100-WRITE-ERROR-LINE
           END-IF.
           IF PD-PRODUCT-VERSION-X-1 NOT NUMERIC
              OR PD-PRODUCT-VERSION-X-2 NOT NUMERIC
               MOVE PROD-VERSION-NOT-NUM-ERR TO DL-ERR-MESSAGE
               PERFORM D100-WRITE-ERROR-LINE
           END-IF.

       C300-VALIDATE-RELEASE-DATE.
           IF PD-RELEASE-DATE = SPACES
               MOVE RELEASE-DATE-MISSING-ERR TO DL-ERR-MESSAGE
               PERFORM D100-WRITE-ERROR-LINE
           ELSE

               IF  PD-RELEASE-DATE-YEAR-X = SPACES
                   OR PD-RELEASE-DATE-MONTH-X = SPACES
                   OR PD-RELEASE-DATE-DAY-X = SPACES
                   MOVE RELEASE-DATE-INCOMPLETE-ERR TO DL-ERR-MESSAGE
                   PERFORM D100-WRITE-ERROR-LINE
               ELSE

                   IF PD-RELEASE-DATE-YEAR-X NOT NUMERIC
                      OR PD-RELEASE-DATE-MONTH-X NOT NUMERIC
                      OR PD-RELEASE-DATE-DAY-X NOT NUMERIC
                      MOVE RELEASE-DATE-NOT-NUM-ERR TO DL-ERR-MESSAGE
                      PERFORM D100-WRITE-ERROR-LINE
                   ELSE

                       IF PD-RELEASE-DATE-YEAR-N > WS-MINIMUM-YEAR
                           AND < WS-MAXIMUM-YEAR
                       CONTINUE
                       ELSE
                           MOVE YEAR-INVALID-ERR TO DL-ERR-MESSAGE
                           PERFORM D100-WRITE-ERROR-LINE

                           IF VALID-MONTH
                               AND 30-DAY-MONTH
                               AND PD-RELEASE-DATE-DAY-N<=30
                               OR VALID-MONTH
                               AND 31-DAY-MONTH
                               AND PD-RELEASE-DATE-DAY-N<=31
                               OR VALID-MONTH
                               AND FEBRUARY
                               AND PD-RELEASE-DATE-DAY-N<=29
                               CONTINUE
                           ELSE
                               MOVE MONTH-INVALID-ERR TO DL-ERR-MESSAGE
                               PERFORM D100-WRITE-ERROR-LINE
                            END-IF
                       END-IF
                   END-IF
               END-IF
           END-IF.

       C400-VALIDATE-NUM-LIC-SOLD.
           IF PD-NUM-LICENSES-SOLD-X = SPACES
               MOVE NUMBER-SOLD-MISSING-ERR TO DL-ERR-MESSAGE
               PERFORM D100-WRITE-ERROR-LINE
           END-IF.

           IF PD-NUM-LICENSES-SOLD-X NOT NUMERIC
               MOVE NUMBER-SOLD-NOT-NUM-ERR TO DL-ERR-MESSAGE
               PERFORM D100-WRITE-ERROR-LINE
           END-IF.

           IF PD-NUMERIC-REL-DATE > WS-TODAYS-DATE
               MOVE NUMBER-SOLD-IN-FUTURE-ERR TO DL-ERR-MESSAGE
               PERFORM D100-WRITE-ERROR-LINE
           END-IF.

       C500-VALIDATE-PROD-SALES.
           IF PD-PRODUCT-VERSION-X-1 < WS-BETA
           AND PD-PRODUCT-SALES-X NOT = SPACES
           AND PD-PRODUCT-SALES-X NOT = ZERO
               MOVE PROD-SALES-INVALID-ERR TO DL-ERR-MESSAGE
               PERFORM D100-WRITE-ERROR-LINE
           END-IF.

           IF PD-NUMERIC-REL-DATE > WS-TODAYS-DATE
           AND PD-PRODUCT-SALES-X NOT = SPACES
               MOVE PROD-SALES-NOT-FUTURE-ERR TO DL-ERR-MESSAGE
               PERFORM D100-WRITE-ERROR-LINE
           END-IF.

           IF PD-PRODUCT-SALES-X NOT NUMERIC
           AND PD-PRODUCT-SALES-X NOT = SPACES
           OR PD-PRODUCT-SALES-X = SPACES
               MOVE PROD-SALES-NOT-NUM-ERR TO DL-ERR-MESSAGE
               PERFORM D100-WRITE-ERROR-LINE
           END-IF.

       C600-VALIDATE-DEVEL-COSTS.
           IF PD-DEVELOPMENT-COST-X = SPACES
               MOVE DEV-COST-MISSING-ERR TO DL-ERR-MESSAGE
               PERFORM D100-WRITE-ERROR-LINE
           END-IF.

           IF PD-DEVELOPMENT-COST-X NOT NUMERIC
               MOVE DEV-COST-NOT-NUM-ERR TO DL-ERR-MESSAGE
               PERFORM D100-WRITE-ERROR-LINE
           END-IF.

       D100-WRITE-ERROR-LINE.
           MOVE "NO" TO WS-VALID-RECORD-SWITCH
               WRITE VALIDATION-AUDIT-RECORD  FROM DETAIL-LINE
                   AFTER ADVANCING WS-LINE-SPACING
               ADD WS-LINE-SPACING TO  WS-CURRENT-LINE-NUMBER
               MOVE 1 TO WS-LINE-SPACING.


----------------------------------------------------------------------------
-------------------

And, here's the input data:
```

#### ↳ Re: Validation Prog. Homework - Help!

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-01-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<786as9$2n5@dfw-ixnews5.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<x6up2.1767$PD.10339027@news.magma.ca>`

```
I haven't studied your code in detail, but the general "philosophy" for
error reports (or screens) where you have to show a "bad" input field's
value is to:

A) REDEFINE the input field as alphanumeric (PIC X) (if it isn't already)

B) Define the place in your output where you show the incorrectly input data
as alphanumeric (PIC X)

C) move the Alphanumeric version of the input field to the alphanumeric
version of the output field - and display (with messages) as alphanumeric

D) Special warning:  You may need to do SOME checking on your invalid
"input" value to make certain it doesn't include non-display characters (for
example "Line Feeds" or "attribute bytes").  In these cases, you will NOT
want to simply move these to your output field - as they could cause your
entire output line/screen to get screwed up.  (This is probably beyond class
assignments - but is worth thinking about.  When your "input" comes from a
"display only" device, this shouldn't be a problem.  However, when this may
occur, you end up having to display error messages with hex-values and more
complex ways of identifying the problem.)
```

```
Use a level 88 definition -

05  some-value   pic 9(03).
    88 some-value-thats-valid value 001 thru 999.


Then say:

   if some-value-thats-valid
      do whatever
   else
      display 'INVALID DATA: '  some-value
   end-if

Jan Lend
```

```
where the requirement did *not* change before the system was up

how to reduce the significance of the change.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Requirements Do Change

_(thread depth: 7)_

- **From:** "Warren Simmons" <w.g.simmons@worldnet.att.net>
- **Date:** 1999-01-23T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<78bkmb$g20@bgtnsc02.worldnet.att.net>`
- **References:** `<x6up2.1767$PD.10339027@news.magma.ca> <36A6B1F3.E54B3525@notayahoo.com> <Ofzp2.1780$PD.10508765@news.magma.ca> <36A73B83.E3B0F2D6@home.com> <Xb5q2.628$Si1.548318@news2.mia> <36A8EA26.72690933@home.com> <78b8ue$50k$1@news.igs.net>`

```
My favorite true story about changes happened at a large lumber company. The
CEO played golf with a consultant from a large DJ 100 company, and talk
turned to how to better use the computers ;they had.

To keep it short, the end result was the lumber company hired the other
company to HELP do a Management Information System.
Every effort was made to do the best possible. All existing management was
placed in a design team, and the assistants took over daily responsiblities.
The consultants taught the design team how to gather the facts, determine
the requirement, and specify the system requirements.  A special hardware
configuration was purchased to support the resulting system(S). In a couple
of years, all of the managers went back to their jobs, and had exactly what
they wanted.

However, the CEO did such a wonderful job that he was promoted to a higher
level, and so were all of the other managers. Quess what the newly appointed
managers wanted?   Changes.  Because they did not work the same as the
people they replaced.

Donald Tees <donald@willmack.com> wrote in message
news:78b8ue$50k$1@news.igs.net...
>Howard Brazee wrote in message <36A8EA26.72690933@home.com>...
>
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Requirements Do Change

_(thread depth: 8)_

- **From:** "Donald Tees" <donald@willmack.com>
- **Date:** 1999-01-23T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<78cb8e$ot9$1@news.igs.net>`

```
Some of the things that always threw me years back (though
now I recognize it and take care of it) are users that lie to
protect their jobs, and users that make things as complex
as they can to look important.  Both can cause problems
between system design and code.

Warren Simmons wrote in message <78bkmb$g20@bgtnsc02.worldnet.att.net>...
>My favorite true story about changes happened at a large lumber company.
The
>CEO played golf with a consultant from a large DJ 100 company, and talk
>turned to how to better use the computers ;they had.
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>placed in a design team, and the assistants took over daily
responsiblities.
>The consultants taught the design team how to gather the facts, determine
>the requirement, and specify the system requirements.  A special hardware
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
>level, and so were all of the other managers. Quess what the newly
appointed
>managers wanted?   Changes.  Because they did not work the same as the
>people they replaced.
…[16 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

_(thread depth: 7)_

- **From:** "Judson McClendon" <judmc123@bellsouth.net>
- **Date:** 1999-01-23T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9zqq2.1146$oi.1094925@news3.mia>`
- **References:** `<x6up2.1767$PD.10339027@news.magma.ca> <36A6B1F3.E54B3525@notayahoo.com> <Ofzp2.1780$PD.10508765@news.magma.ca> <36A73B83.E3B0F2D6@home.com> <Xb5q2.628$Si1.548318@news2.mia> <36A8EA26.72690933@home.com> <78b8ue$50k$1@news.igs.net>`

```
Donald Tees wrote:
>
>Howard Brazee wrote:
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
>how to reduce the significance of the change.

About the only thing I have found that really helps is building into the
system as much flexibility as possible.  Not perfect, but after a while
you get an idea of what kind of changes you may be faced with.  If you
build in enough flexibility, the impact of those changes is reduced.
```

```
Re flexibility:  only paint yourself into corners where at least one of the
walls (or a low ceiling!) has a door or a window, and be _very_ careful in
burning any bridges behind you.
```

```
Howard Brazee wrote in message <36A73B83.E3B0F2D6@home.com>...
>> This is only the fourth COBOL program I've ever written, so please excuse
>> the terrible form, etc.  I think I've got it working now  though.
However,
>> i wish had more time until the assignment was due because I'd like to
>> totally recode it now that I know what I'm doing ;)
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>the temptation!

I've often said that I have never "finished" a program.  It seems that there
is always something more that could be done to make a program "better", but
reality dictates that you stop the "improvement" process somewhere along the
way and release the program for distribution/production.
```

<a id="t-09fb2fba13"></a>
## Thread: Cobol application running on NT4 server & clients — 4 msgs

### Cobol application running on NT4 server & clients

- **From:** "Stevo" <s_stevo@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 1999-01-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<785d9q$kdd$1@news1.skynet.be>`

```
I work on application that was originally intended for unix and DOS
(Windows) .
The application is rather large (> 500 programs), the compiler used is from
MicroFocus (.int - files run with cobrun)

Since november this year we adapted it slightly so it runs also in a full NT
environment in a DOS-only environment.  Internal test showed that it runs
but it is 2 times slower than under win98.

Anyone that encountered this problem. Is it a setting in NT?

Best regards,

Desperate stevo.
```

#### ↳ Re: Cobol application running on NT4 server & clients

- **From:** redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1999-01-21T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36a69f64.169740248@news1.ibm.net>`
- **References:** `<785d9q$kdd$1@news1.skynet.be>`

```
On Wed, 20 Jan 1999 21:17:45 +0100, "Stevo" <s_stevo@hotmail.com>
wrote:

>I work on application that was originally intended for unix and DOS
>(Windows) .
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
>Desperate stevo.

Steve, you have to remember that unless you compiled it 32 Bit - NT
has to do a lot of 16 bit Emulation - Unlike Windows 95/95 (Which is
still a 32 bit hack on top of a 16 bit OS).  

One thing that MIGHT help - and I'd appreciate you posting your
results is to use: FORCEDOS yourprogram
on the command line.

That assumes you are still compiling it for DOS and not Windows.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Cobol application running on NT4 server & clients

- **From:** "Stevo" <s_stevo@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 1999-01-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<78f22n$r31$1@news0.skynet.be>`
- **References:** `<785d9q$kdd$1@news1.skynet.be> <36a69f64.169740248@news1.ibm.net>`

```
Thanks for your input on this matter :

FORCEDOS makes it possible to run the program
as well as DOSONLY.  I haven't found any difference yet.  What the averall
speed is concerned I found out that with a lot of users the cause laid in
malconfigured virusscanners, schedulers , ...

I was wondering if it isn't possible to get out of  that dosonly environment
to start for eg Word of Excell??
and than return to my 16bit application.

----
Compiling the application for NT (dll, gnt) is at this stage not really an
option.  We have to stay compatible with our DOS and WIN95/98, Novell users
and adding an other version complicates this too much.
(already support for 2 languages, 2 types of OS : unix sco - dos/windows)



Thane Hubbell heeft geschreven in bericht
<36a69f64.169740248@news1.ibm.net>...
>On Wed, 20 Jan 1999 21:17:45 +0100, "Stevo" <s_stevo@hotmail.com>
>wrote:
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>>The application is rather large (> 500 programs), the compiler used is
from
>>MicroFocus (.int - files run with cobrun)
>>
>>Since november this year we adapted it slightly so it runs also in a full
NT
>>environment in a DOS-only environment.  Internal test showed that it runs
>>but it is 2 times slower than under win98.
…[16 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

#### ↳ Re: Cobol application running on NT4 server & clients

- **From:** "J���rgen Ibelgaufts" <ibelgaufts@gfc-net.de>
- **Date:** 1999-01-21T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36A72813.A268B080@gfc-net.de>`
- **References:** `<785d9q$kdd$1@news1.skynet.be>`

```
Hi,

I wonder why you use the .int format and not the .gnt format (or .dll on windows
nt). gnt and dll are ***MUCH*** faster (I experienced 20 times in some cases) in
case you use a 32 bit Cobol. All you have to do is change the compiler option to
omf"gnt" or use the CBLLINK program with the option to create a .dll (on windows)

Hope this helps
Juergen Ibelgaufts

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Stevo schrieb:
> 
> I work on application that was originally intended for unix and DOS
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
> Desperate stevo.
```

## Thread: Urgent : problem .... — 4 msgs

_Topics: [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Urgent : problem ....

- **From:** "joris.poelmans" <9410355@student.luc.ac.be>
- **Date:** 1999-01-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Pine.A41.4.05.9901201242460.91350-100000@rsftew.luc.ac.be>`

```
The following program compiles in a normal way, but at run time it gives
the following message 

"163     Illegal character in numeric field"

... I'm using ansi cobol85 with a tbox compiler.

       identification division.
       program-id. sportp51.
      *author-id. poelmans.

       environment division.
       input-output section.
       file-control.
        select sport assign to "sport.fle".
        select lijst assign to "lijst1.fle".
       
       data division.
       file section.
       fd sport.
          01 sport-record.
             02 lidno pic x(2).
             02 filler pic x.
             02 naam pic x(30).
             02 filler pic x.
             02 gebjaar pic 9(2).
             02 sporttak pic 9.
             02 filler pic x(2).                                       

         fd lijst.
          01 lijst-record.
             02 lidno1 pic x(2).
             02 filler pic x.
             02 naam1 pic x.
             02 filler pic x.
             02 lidgeld pic 9(3).9.    

           working-storage section.
       01 einde-bestand pic x value "n".
       01 sportkeuze pic 9.
          88 tennis  value 1.
          88 squash  value 2.
          88 fitness  value 3.
       01 prijs pic 9(3)v9.
       01 korting pic v999 value 0.125.
       01 leeftijd pic 9(2).
       01 tennistel pic 9(3) value 0.
       01 squashtel pic 9(3) value 0.
       01 fitnesstel pic 9(3) value 0.
       01 datum.
          02 jaar pic 9(2).
          02 maand pic 9(2).
          02 dag pic 9(2).        

       procedure division.

       hoofdprog.
       open input sport
       open output lijst
       perform verwerk  until einde-bestand = "j"
       close sport
       close lijst
       display  "Aantal leden"
       display  "************"
       display  "Tennis :" tennistel
       display  "Squash :" squashtel
       display  "Fitness :" fitnesstel
       stop run. 

       verwerk.
       read sport at end move 'j' to  einde-bestand
         not at end
              if tennis
                  add  1 to tennistel
                 move 1500 to prijs
                 else if squash
                          add 1 to squashtel
                         move 1200 to prijs
                         else add 1 to fitnesstel
                              move 1000 to  prijs
                      end-if
               end-if   
         accept datum from date
               compute leeftijd = jaar  - gebjaar
               if leeftijd < 16
                  compute prijs = prijs * (1 - korting)
                  end-if
               move lidno to lidno1
               move naam to naam1
               move prijs to lidgeld
               write lijst-record before advancing 1 line end-write
       end-read.         


The fle file looks like this

10 Vlinders Annelies              87 1
11 Merels Karel                   65 1
12 Valkenborgs Miet               80 2
13 Parelyn Peter                  78 2
14 Koninkjes Elisa                69 3
15 Pingeon Gert                   86 3    

Please help,  greetings,

Joris  

       
     *******************************************************
     *             Joris.Poelmans@advalvas.be              *    
     *     J-Tech Invest : http://lisa.luc.ac.be/jtech     *
     *                The truth is a virus                 *     
     *******************************************************
```

#### ↳ Re: Urgent : problem ....

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-01-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<784u6k$2t1@sjx-ixn10.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<Pine.A41.4.05.9901201242460.91350-100000@rsftew.luc.ac.be>`

```
I don't think that you SHOULD have to do this, but try adding a VALUE 0 to
leeftijd (just as you do for most of your other numeric fields).  You don't
initialize it before having it be the receiving item of a compute statement.

P.S.  For those who don't know it, "tbox" is probably an old Micro Focus
"Toolbox" which a step of from their compiler-only product on Unix.   I
think it includes an Animator, so, Joris, if this doesn't fix your problem,
you should run it thru Animator and see where the exact problem (nonnumeric
data in a numeric field) problem is.
```

#### ↳ Re: Urgent : problem ....

- **From:** swiegand@neo.rr.com (SkippyPB)
- **Date:** 1999-01-20T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<4822AAD15BAD7007.2FB238AFFC37220C.4CA546991786033E@library-proxy.airnews.net>`
- **References:** `<Pine.A41.4.05.9901201242460.91350-100000@rsftew.luc.ac.be>`

```
On Wed, 20 Jan 1999 12:50:41 +0100, "joris.poelmans"
<9410355@student.luc.ac.be> enlightened us:

>The following program compiles in a normal way, but at run time it gives
>the following message 
…[110 more quoted lines elided]…
>     *******************************************************

I think your file definition doesn't match your data.  Your FD is:

fd sport.
          01 sport-record.
             02 lidno pic x(2).
             02 filler pic x.
             02 naam pic x(30).
             02 filler pic x.
             02 gebjaar pic 9(2).
             02 sporttak pic 9.
             02 filler pic x(2).          

And the data is:

10 Vlinders Annelies              87 1
11 Merels Karel                   65 1
12 Valkenborgs Miet               80 2
13 Parelyn Peter                  78 2
14 Koninkjes Elisa                69 3
15 Pingeon Gert                   86 3    

You define GEBJAAR followed by SPORTTAK, one  PIC 99, the other PIC 9.
It appears from the data you provided that there should be a FILLER of
Pic X defined in between these two fields.

HTH


          ////
         (o o)
-oOO--(_)--OOo-

It may be that your sole purpose in life is simply to serve as a warning to 
others.

 Steve
```

#### ↳ Re: Urgent : problem ....

- **From:** "COBOL Frog (Huib Klink)" <H.Klink@IMN.nl>
- **Date:** 1999-01-22T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36A85156.9298D53E@IMN.nl>`
- **References:** `<Pine.A41.4.05.9901201242460.91350-100000@rsftew.luc.ac.be>`

```
You don't MOVE SPORTTAK TO SPORTKEUZE, so sportkeuze is uninitialized,
probably containing low-value (x'00'). The IF TENNIS, a numeric compare,
will give error 163 then. Either add the move, or (simplier) putt the three
88-levels behind SPORTTAK and remove SPORTKEUZE from working-storage.

There are other errors, see post of SkippyPB ...

I advise to use write AFTER advancing. Somehow, at least in complex outputs,
this is easier to control. ('cause the amount to advance is usually known
for the current line to be printed, not for the future coming line).

Success. The Frog

joris.poelmans wrote:

> The following program compiles in a normal way, but at run time it gives
> the following message
…[109 more quoted lines elided]…
>      *******************************************************
```

```
Hello all, I'm trolling for people looking for work (and no DD, I will not
post rates, they are widely varied depending on position, anywhere from
30k-120k).

We have many positions available to include Systems Programmers, Database
Administrators (IMS and DB2), Application Programmers with Cobol or Assembler.
A knowledge of MVS, JCL, CICS, IMS, DB2 would be nice, but we also need entry
level people.

Rates depend on experience and are competitive, include great benefits and
stock in a low cost of living area (Southeast US mostly).

If anyone is interested, please give me a mail and a resume at
'zberger@ldl.net'.
```

## Thread: Van viscobol 3,3 naar??? — 14 msgs

_Topics: [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Van viscobol 3,3 naar???

- **From:** "J.Holtrigter" <j.holtrigter@inter.nl.net>
- **Date:** 1999-01-21T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<787o6m$f2g$1@news.Kijfhoek.NL.net>`

```
Wij gebruiken viscobol 3.3 16 bits netwerkversie. We hebben een upgrade
inhuis voor 3.5 16 bits netwerkversie. Van deze versie stopt de
ondersteuning. Overgang naar Netexpres is mogelijk, echter:

Er draaien novell servers, hieraan gekoppeld zijn pc's onder dos 6.22
en pc's onder w95 waar de applicatie draait in een dosbox.

Op pc onder dos kunnen geen 32 bits versies draaien (zoals de nieuwe van
netexpress).

Wat we zoeken is een 16 bits cobol die in ieder geval qua functionaliteit
(denk aan zaken als toegestane bestandsgrootte e.d.) , stabiliteit en
performance gelijkwaardig is aan viscobol 3.3 en dit ook heeft bewezen en
waar ondersteuning voor is.

Wie weet een oplossing?
```

#### ↳ Re: Van viscobol 3,3 naar???

- **From:** Albert Ratzlaff <albert@conexion.com.py>
- **Date:** 1999-01-22T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

- **References:** `<787o6m$f2g$1@news.Kijfhoek.NL.net>`

```
Lets give it a try (I know german, but not dutch or afrikaans).

"J.Holtrigter" wrote:

> Wij gebruiken viscobol 3.3 16 bits netwerkversie. We hebben een upgrade
> inhuis voor 3.5 16 bits netwerkversie. Van deze versie stopt de
> ondersteuning. Overgang naar Netexpres is mogelijk, echter:

We are using viscobol 3.3 16 bits network version. We have upgraded ?? to
network version 3.5 16 bits. From this version on the ?? stops. Migration to
Netexpress is posible, but:

> Er draaien novell servers, hieraan gekoppeld zijn pc's onder dos 6.22
> en pc's onder w95 waar de applicatie draait in een dosbox.

We have PCs with DOS 6.22 and PCs with Windows 95 using the application in a
DOS box connected to Novell servers.

> Op pc onder dos kunnen geen 32 bits versies draaien (zoals de nieuwe van
> netexpress).

??

> Wat we zoeken is een 16 bits cobol die in ieder geval qua functionaliteit
> (denk aan zaken als toegestane bestandsgrootte e.d.) , stabiliteit en
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> Wie weet een oplossing?

What we are looking for is a 16 Bit COBOL with the equivalent functionality,
stability and performance of Viscobol 3.3 and also that that has been
demostrated (I can't place: ondersteuning) ??.

Who knows a solution?

Regards
Albert Ratzlaff
(hope no one get's upset at me for this ;)
```

#### ↳ Re: Van viscobol 3,3 naar???

- **From:** "COBOL Frog (Huib Klink)" <H.Klink@IMN.nl>
- **Date:** 1999-01-22T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36A84ABC.A5620622@IMN.nl>`
- **References:** `<787o6m$f2g$1@news.Kijfhoek.NL.net>`

```
Hier is de vertaling:
Here is the translation:

J.Holtrigter schreef in gewoon nederlands:
J.Holtrigter wrote in plain Dutch:

> Wij gebruiken viscobol 3.3 16 bits netwerkversie. We hebben een upgrade
> inhuis voor 3.5 16 bits netwerkversie. Van deze versie stopt de
> ondersteuning. Overgang naar Netexpres is mogelijk, echter:

We use viscobol 3.3 16 bits network version. We have an upgrade
in stock to 3.5 16 bit network version. This version's support stops.
Switch to Netexpres is possible, but:

> Er draaien novell servers, hieraan gekoppeld zijn pc's onder dos 6.22
> en pc's onder w95 waar de applicatie draait in een dosbox.

There are novell servers, attthed to them zre pc's under dos 6.22
and pc's under w95 with the application running in a dos box.

> Op pc onder dos kunnen geen 32 bits versies draaien (zoals de nieuwe van
> netexpress).

On pc under dos it is not poossible to run 32 bit versions (like the new ones

from netexpress).

> Wat we zoeken is een 16 bits cobol die in ieder geval qua functionaliteit
> (denk aan zaken als toegestane bestandsgrootte e.d.) , stabiliteit en
> performance gelijkwaardig is aan viscobol 3.3 en dit ook heeft bewezen en
> waar ondersteuning voor is.

What we are looking for is a 16 bit cobol that at least is equal in
functionality
(with things as allowed file size etc.), stability and
performance to viscobol 3.3 and where this is proven to be and
which has support for.

> Wie weet een oplossing?

Who knows a solution?

-------------------------
Translated by De Kikker
```
